values.value:
== DESTRUCTIVE CHANGES FORMAT == Use destructiveFiles array for deletes. The xmlContent is a destructiveChangesPost.xml: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <Package xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <types> <members>ObjectName__c.FieldName__c</members> <members>ObjectName__c</members> <name>CustomField</name> </types> <version>61.0</version> </Package> RULE: For deleting an object, first list all its custom fields as CustomField members, then add the object itself as a CustomObject member — Salesforce requires relationship fields to be deleted before the object.
== CRITICAL RULES ==
== ABSOLUTE CSV RULE — HIGHEST PRIORITY == IF the ticket has ANY CSV file attachment: → You MUST use dmlOperations with type csvProcess → You MUST NOT generate any XML files for the CSV data → You MUST NOT guess or assume CSV content → The files array MUST be empty []
1. NEVER create a field that already exists (check org state) 2. NEVER create an object that already exists (check org state) 3. For field UPDATES: read the existing field values from org state, preserve all unchanged values 4. MasterDetail sharingModel rule: If an object HAS or IS GETTING a MasterDetail field, ALWAYS set sharingModel=ControlledByParent — even when you are only adding unrelated fields. 5. Multiple fields on same object = ONE object XML file with multiple <fields> blocks 6. When adding fields to an existing object: use exact label/pluralLabel/nameField from org state 7. Return ONLY the JSON object. No explanation, no markdown code fences, no preamble.
== CRITICAL SHARING MODEL RULE == Before generating ANY CustomObject XML wrapper, check the org state for existing MasterDetail fields. If the object has ANY MasterDetail field: → sharingModel MUST be ControlledByParent → This applies even if you are only adding a Text or Date field NEVER use ReadWrite, Read, or Private on an object that has or may have a MasterDetail field.
CRITICAL: Custom Metadata Types (__mdt) and Custom Settings (__c) are COMPLETELY DIFFERENT: - Custom Metadata Type: uses <label>, <pluralLabel>, <visibility> — NO <customSettingsType> - Custom Setting: uses <label>, <customSettingsType> — NO <pluralLabel>, NO <visibility> - NEVER mix these two XML formats CRITICAL: There is NO <customMetadataType> XML element. This tag does NOT exist. The ONLY way Salesforce knows it is a Custom Metadata Type is because the API name ends in __mdt. Never add <customMetadataType>, <customSettingsType>, or any other discriminator tag to __mdt objects.
Critical rules: CSV handling, destructive changes, sharing model, duplicate fields
